---
title: "B1 anthocyanin mapping benchmark (DEFERRED)"
subtitle: "Discussion / future application — mapping power & resolution across callers × sources"
author: "Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

::: {.callout-important title="STATUS: DEFERRED (B2.4)"}
The B1 phenotype table + NIL panel are **not ready** (per user). This note plans
the pipeline abstractly now; it becomes a real analysis once the B1 phenotype
and panel are located, otherwise it stays a "future application" paragraph in the
Discussion.
:::

# Planned pipeline (abstract)

1. **Project decoded ancestry → mapping genotype.** Convert each caller's
   REF/HET/ALT segments to a segment/dosage genotype — the mapping unit implied
   by the zygosity-identifiability ceiling (memory
   `snp50k-zygosity-identifiability`, `gatk-vs-posterior-genotype-calling`).
2. **Map the B1 QTL** (anthocyanin) per (caller × source) call set.
3. **Compare mapping power/resolution** — QTL detection, support-interval width,
   effect-size stability — across callers and sequencing modalities.

# Why it matters

This is the downstream payoff: do the simulation-calibrated calls (B2.2) and the
cheaper modality (BrB/skim vs target-seq) recover the same QTL at comparable
resolution? A "yes" closes the loop from calibration → concordance → application.

# Inputs still needed

- B1 anthocyanin phenotype table (sample → phenotype).
- The NIL panel genotyped for B1 (overlap with the paired cohort in B2.3).
